1	"""Methodology Quality Score (MQS): operational form of the five working
2	criteria from THE_META_METHOD.md.
3	
4	Each Conclusion that has at least one MethodologyProfile gets an MQS row with
5	five sub-scores in [0,1], an LLM-or-stub-judged evidence blob per sub-score,
6	and a composite that uses Domain Sensitivity as a multiplicative gate.
7	
8	The composite formula and sub-score rubrics are documented in
9	docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md. The doc and this module are kept in sync
10	by scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import json
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass
18	from datetime import datetime, timezone
19	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Protocol
20	
21	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
22	
23	
24	MQS_SCHEMA = "theseus.mqs.v1"
25	PROMPT_VERSION = "mqs-prompt-v1.0"
26	
27	# Mirrored verbatim by docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md and verified by
28	# scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
29	COMPOSITE_FORMULA = (
30	    "domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)"
31	)
32	
33	SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS: dict[str, float] = {
34	    "progressivity": 0.25,
35	    "severity": 0.25,
36	    "aim_method_fit": 0.25,
37	    "compressibility": 0.25,
38	}
39	
40	_DECISION_RULE_RE = re.compile(
41	    r"\b(if [a-z]+.*?then |we will |we'll |exit if |by [0-9]{4}|by q[1-4] |"
42	    r"check back |trigger |would falsif|will revisit)",
43	    re.IGNORECASE,
44	)
45	_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP = 600
46	
47	
48	class MqsJudge(Protocol):
49	    """LLM-judge contract. Tests pass a stub; production wires the real LLM."""
50	
51	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
52	        """Return a dict with at least {"score": float in [0,1], "rationale": str}."""
53	        ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class StubMqsJudge:
58	    """Deterministic judge used by tests and by the no-LLM backfill path.
59	
60	    `responses` lets a test pin a per-criterion score; otherwise every call
61	    returns `default_score` with rationale "stub".
62	    """
63	
64	    responses: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None = None
65	    default_score: float = 0.5
66	
67	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
68	        if self.responses and criterion in self.responses:
69	            return self.responses[criterion]
70	        return {"score": self.default_score, "rationale": "stub"}
71

111	def _is_postgres_url(url: str) -> bool:

115	def _psycopg2_compatible_url(url: str) -> str:
116	    """Remove client-only pooler hints that psycopg2 rejects."""
117	
118	    if "?" not in url or not _is_postgres_url(url):
119	        return url
120	    parts = urlsplit(url)
121	    filtered = [
122	        (key, value)
123	        for key, value in parse_qsl(parts.query, keep_blank_values=True)
124	        if key.lower() not in _PSYCOPG2_UNSUPPORTED_QUERY_PARAMS
125	    ]
126	    return urlunsplit(
127	        (parts.scheme, parts.netloc, parts.path, urlencode(filtered), parts.fragment)
128	    )

2	
3	Goals:
4	1. Domain Sensitivity gate: a low domain-fit score caps the composite even
5	   when every other criterion is high.
6	2. Deterministic stub judge produces stable scores (so backfill is auditable
7	   without an LLM in the loop).
8	3. The evidence blob serializes and deserializes through JSON without loss
9	   (this is the contract the Prisma `Json` column relies on).
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import json
15	
16	from noosphere.evaluation.mqs import (
17	    COMPOSITE_FORMULA,
18	    MQS_SCHEMA,
19	    MethodologyProfileSummary,
20	    MqsInput,
21	    StubMqsJudge,
22	    composite_score,
23	    evidence_payload,
24	    evidence_payload_json,
25	    score_conclusion,
26	    score_progressivity,
27	)
28	
29	
30	def _profile(**overrides) -> MethodologyProfileSummary:
31	    base = dict(
32	        pattern_type="empirical_calibration",
33	        title="Empirical calibration",
34	        summary="Asks what evidence would discipline the belief.",
35	        reasoning_moves=["convert posture into evidence thresholds"],
36	        transfer_targets=["forecasting"],
37	        assumptions=["a serious belief should expose what would defeat it"],
38	        failure_modes=["can quantify false precision when channel is thin"],
39	        confidence=0.7,
40	    )
41	    base.update(overrides)
42	    return MethodologyProfileSummary(**base)
43	
44	
45	def test_domain_sensitivity_acts_as_gate_on_composite() -> None:
46	    """A near-zero domain sensitivity must cap composite to near zero, even
47	    when every other criterion is at the maximum."""
48	    judge = StubMqsJudge(
49	        responses={
50	            "severity": {"score": 1.0, "rationale": "very severe"},
51	            "aim_method_fit": {"score": 1.0, "rationale": "fits"},
52	            "compressibility": {"score": 1.0, "rationale": "single assumption"},
53	            "domain_sensitivity": {"score": 0.05, "rationale": "out of domain"},
54	        }
55	    )
56	    out = score_conclusion(
57	        MqsInput(
58	            conclusion_id="c1",
59	            conclusion_text="we will exit if monthly active users falls below 10k",
60	            rationale="if growth flattens then thesis breaks",
61	            topic_hint="forecasting",
62	            profiles=[_profile()],
63	            forecast_count=2,
64	            has_check_back_date=True,
65	            dissent_claim_count=1,
66	        ),
67	        judge=judge,
68	    )
69	
70	    # All other sub-scores high.
71	    assert out.progressivity.score >= 0.85
72	    assert out.severity.score >= 0.5
73	    assert out.aim_method_fit.score >= 0.9
74	    assert out.compressibility.score == 1.0
75	
76	    # Domain sensitivity gate clamps the composite.
77	    assert out.domain_sensitivity.score <= 0.1
78	    assert out.composite <= 0.1, (
79	        f"Domain sensitivity gate failed: composite={out.composite}, "
80	        f"domain={out.domain_sensitivity.score}"
81	    )

summary: Inspect existing methods and forecast infrastructure

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my final report:

## Infrastructure Analysis Summary

I've thoroughly explored the Theseus repository to understand the existing infrastructure for method-outcome linkage implementation. Here's the breakdown:

### 1. Methods Registry (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/methods/_registry.py:26-91`)

**Structure:** Decorator-based registry keyed on `(name, version)` tuples.
- **Method fields:** `name`, `version`, `status` (experimental/active/deprecated/retired), plus implementation reference and schema details
- **Registry API:** `register(spec, fn)`, `get(name, version="latest")`, `list(status_filter=None)`, `set_emits_edges(method_id, edges)`, `get_emits_edges(method_id)`
- **Version selection:** Semantic versioning with "latest" defaulting to the highest non-retired version
- **Iteration:** `list()` returns all specs; filter by status applied at call time

### 2. Resolution Tracker (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/resolution_tracker.py:37-261`)

**Captures:** `ForecastResolution` rows per prediction with:
- `prediction_id`, `market_outcome` (YES/NO/CANCELLED), `brier_score`, `log_loss`, `calibration_bucket`
- `resolved_at` timestamp, `justification` text, `raw_settlement` JSON
- Idempotent append-only writes by prediction_id
- Portfolio calibration computed from 90-day rolling window (line:31, SCORE_WINDOW)

### 3. Schema (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma`)

**Current models: 40 total** (none named `ConclusionMethod` or `MethodTrackRecord` — confirmed clear)

**Key relationships:**
- **Conclusion** (line:432-487): Has `1:1` optional `MethodologyQualityScore`, `1:N` `MethodologyProfile`, `1:N` `PublicationReview`
- **MethodologyProfile** (line:592-628): Links to `Upload` OR `Conclusion` (never both), has `sourceKind`, `patternType`, `title`, JSON fields for `reasoningMoves`, `transferTargets`, `assumptions`, `failureModes`
- **MethodologyQualityScore** (line:643-675): `1:1` with Conclusion via `@unique conclusionId`, holds five sub-scores (progressivity, severity, aimMethodFit, compressibility, domainSensitivity), composite, and JSON evidence blob
- **ForecastPrediction** (line:874-905): Links to `ForecastMarket`, has `probabilityYes`, `status`, `headline`, `reasoning`, `modelName`, `1:1` optional `ForecastResolution`
- **ForecastResolution** (line:945-961): `1:1` with Prediction via `@unique predictionId`, stores `marketOutcome`, `brierScore`, `logLoss`, `calibrationBucket`, `resolvedAt`

### 4. Forecast Generator (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/forecast_generator.py:611-741`)

**Flow:** Market → retrieve sources → Haiku LLM (strict JSON schema) → validate citations verbatim → write `ForecastPrediction` + `ForecastCitation` rows, or abstain with outcome enum.
- Traces: `_write_forecast_trace()` (line:578-600) writes `ForecastTrace` with `principlesUsed` (citation IDs + weights), `modelOutput`, `gateResults`
- Paper bets evaluated via `evaluate_and_stake()` (line:727-732)

### 5. Portfolio Surface (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts:245-429`)

**Return shape:** `ForecastPortfolioSurface` with:
- `mode` (PAPER/LIVE/GATE-BLOCKED), `kpis` (openPositions, realizedPaperPnl, unrealizedPaperPnl, runningBrier, hitRate)
- `openPositions`, `recentlyResolved`, `pipeline` arrays, `watching` state
- Helpers: `normalizeTracePrinciples()`, `normalizeGateResults()`, `marketUrl()`, `decimalNumber()`, `money()`
- Aggregation: Brier computed from 25 most recent resolved; 90d mean fallback from `ForecastPortfolioState`

### 6. CLI Commands (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/cli_commands/methods.py`)

**Framework:** Click (not Typer). Commands:
- `list`: `REGISTRY.list(status_filter)` → rich table or JSON
- `show`: `REGISTRY.get(name, version)` → method details
- `run`: `REGISTRY.get()` → `fn(payload)` → result
- `diff`: Compare two versions field-by-field
- `extract-candidates`: Filter experimental methods
- **DB connection:** Lazy `_get_store()` → `get_orchestrator().store` (line:15-17)

### 7. Methods Page (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/methods/[name]/[version]/page.tsx`)

**Structure:** Server-side RSC. Loads via `fetchMethodVersion(organizationId, name, version)` (scoped to org). UI: Metadata card + "Package Method" and "Document Method" server actions with ledger entry redirection.

### 8. MQS Module (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/evaluation/mqs.py:1-100`)

**Severity sub-score:** Part of five-criterion evaluation. `StubMqsJudge` (line:57-70) provides deterministic testing; production uses LLM judge via `judge(criterion, prompt)` protocol (line:48-53).
- Evidence serialized as JSON blob (line:659 in schema)
- Composite formula: `domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)` (line:29-31)

### 9. DB Connection from Python

**Pattern:** SQLModel (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy) with direct Postgres. Store (`noosphere/store.py:1-150`) uses:
- `SQLModel` base with `SQLField` + SQLAlchemy Column definitions
- `sqlmodel.Session` for transactions
- Models imported into `noosphere/models.py` (lines:29-102)
- No ORM layer; raw SQL only for complex joins
- `create_engine()` with pooling for Supabase pooler compatibility (line:140-150)

### 10. Test Structure

**Pattern:** Pytest fixtures + deterministic mocks. Example `test_mqs.py`:
- Fixture-based: `_profile(**overrides)` factory (line:30-42)
- Mock judge: `StubMqsJudge(responses={}, default_score=0.5)` (line:48-54)
- Assertions on JSON serialization and composite formula (line:45-81)
- No database needed for most tests (stub judge + in-memory data)

---

**Summary for Implementation:** You have SQLModel/Prisma dual-stack: Python side uses SQLModel for serialization/validation, Portal side uses Prisma ORM. Forecast prediction → resolution → MQS pipeline is established. Method registry is decorator-driven (name+version keyed). The new `ConclusionMethod` (M:N Conclusion↔Method) and `MethodTrackRecord` (1:1 Conclusion or ForecastPrediction linkage to track record) models will slot in as Prisma models with corresponding SQLModel mirrors in Python.

I need to understand existing infrastructure in the Theseus repo at /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus before implementing a method-outcome linkage feature. Please report:

1. **Methods registry**: Read `noosphere/noosphere/methods/_registry.py` — describe the registry structure, the decorator system, what fields each registered method has (name, slug, domain, etc.), and how methods are listed/iterated.

2. **Resolution tracker**: Read `noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/resolution_tracker.py` — describe how resolutions are captured, what data they record (probability/outcome/score/etc.).

3. **Schema**: Read `theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma` — show me the full models for `ForecastPrediction`, `ForecastResolution`, `MethodologyProfile`, `Conclusion`. Especially how Conclusion relates to MethodologyProfile, and how ForecastPrediction/Resolution relate to Conclusion. Also note the current model count and any existing models named `ConclusionMethod` or `MethodTrackRecord` (we are adding these — confirm they DO NOT already exist).

4. **Forecast generator**: Read `noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/forecast_generator.py` — describe how conclusions become forecast predictions.

5. **Portfolio surface**: Read `theseus-codex/src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts` — describe the aggregation pattern (return shape, helper functions).

6. **CLI commands**: Read `noosphere/noosphere/cli_commands/methods.py` — describe the existing CLI structure (Click? Typer?), existing subcommands, how it connects to Prisma/DB.

7. **Methods page**: Read `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/methods/[name]/page.tsx` — describe its current structure, what data it loads.

8. **MQS module**: Check if `noosphere/noosphere/evaluation/mqs.py` exists and briefly describe its severity sub-score (we need to know how it's structured to potentially couple it with track record).

9. **DB connection from Python**: Show me how Python code in `noosphere/` reads/writes to the Prisma DB — does it use direct Postgres? An ORM? A specific helper module? Look at any existing evaluation module.

10. **Tests**: How are tests structured under `noosphere/tests/`? Look at `noosphere/tests/test_mqs.py` and any other recent test to understand the pattern (pytest? fixtures? mock DB?).

Report under 600 words. Use file:line references. I need enough detail to plan the implementation but don't need every line of code.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my final report:

## Infrastructure Analysis Summary

I've thoroughly explored the Theseus repository to understand the existing infrastructure for method-outcome linkage implementation. Here's the breakdown:

### 1. Methods Registry (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/methods/_registry.py:26-91`)

**Structure:** Decorator-based registry keyed on `(name, version)` tuples.
- **Method fields:** `name`, `version`, `status` (experimental/active/deprecated/retired), plus implementation reference and schema details
- **Registry API:** `register(spec, fn)`, `get(name, version="latest")`, `list(status_filter=None)`, `set_emits_edges(method_id, edges)`, `get_emits_edges(method_id)`
- **Version selection:** Semantic versioning with "latest" defaulting to the highest non-retired version
- **Iteration:** `list()` returns all specs; filter by status applied at call time

### 2. Resolution Tracker (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/resolution_tracker.py:37-261`)

**Captures:** `ForecastResolution` rows per prediction with:
- `prediction_id`, `market_outcome` (YES/NO/CANCELLED), `brier_score`, `log_loss`, `calibration_bucket`
- `resolved_at` timestamp, `justification` text, `raw_settlement` JSON
- Idempotent append-only writes by prediction_id
- Portfolio calibration computed from 90-day rolling window (line:31, SCORE_WINDOW)

### 3. Schema (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma`)

**Current models: 40 total** (none named `ConclusionMethod` or `MethodTrackRecord` — confirmed clear)

**Key relationships:**
- **Conclusion** (line:432-487): Has `1:1` optional `MethodologyQualityScore`, `1:N` `MethodologyProfile`, `1:N` `PublicationReview`
- **MethodologyProfile** (line:592-628): Links to `Upload` OR `Conclusion` (never both), has `sourceKind`, `patternType`, `title`, JSON fields for `reasoningMoves`, `transferTargets`, `assumptions`, `failureModes`
- **MethodologyQualityScore** (line:643-675): `1:1` with Conclusion via `@unique conclusionId`, holds five sub-scores (progressivity, severity, aimMethodFit, compressibility, domainSensitivity), composite, and JSON evidence blob
- **ForecastPrediction** (line:874-905): Links to `ForecastMarket`, has `probabilityYes`, `status`, `headline`, `reasoning`, `modelName`, `1:1` optional `ForecastResolution`
- **ForecastResolution** (line:945-961): `1:1` with Prediction via `@unique predictionId`, stores `marketOutcome`, `brierScore`, `logLoss`, `calibrationBucket`, `resolvedAt`

### 4. Forecast Generator (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/forecast_generator.py:611-741`)

**Flow:** Market → retrieve sources → Haiku LLM (strict JSON schema) → validate citations verbatim → write `ForecastPrediction` + `ForecastCitation` rows, or abstain with outcome enum.
- Traces: `_write_forecast_trace()` (line:578-600) writes `ForecastTrace` with `principlesUsed` (citation IDs + weights), `modelOutput`, `gateResults`
- Paper bets evaluated via `evaluate_and_stake()` (line:727-732)

### 5. Portfolio Surface (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts:245-429`)

**Return shape:** `ForecastPortfolioSurface` with:
- `mode` (PAPER/LIVE/GATE-BLOCKED), `kpis` (openPositions, realizedPaperPnl, unrealizedPaperPnl, runningBrier, hitRate)
- `openPositions`, `recentlyResolved`, `pipeline` arrays, `watching` state
- Helpers: `normalizeTracePrinciples()`, `normalizeGateResults()`, `marketUrl()`, `decimalNumber()`, `money()`
- Aggregation: Brier computed from 25 most recent resolved; 90d mean fallback from `ForecastPortfolioState`

### 6. CLI Commands (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/cli_commands/methods.py`)

**Framework:** Click (not Typer). Commands:
- `list`: `REGISTRY.list(status_filter)` → rich table or JSON
- `show`: `REGISTRY.get(name, version)` → method details
- `run`: `REGISTRY.get()` → `fn(payload)` → result
- `diff`: Compare two versions field-by-field
- `extract-candidates`: Filter experimental methods
- **DB connection:** Lazy `_get_store()` → `get_orchestrator().store` (line:15-17)

### 7. Methods Page (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/methods/[name]/[version]/page.tsx`)

**Structure:** Server-side RSC. Loads via `fetchMethodVersion(organizationId, name, version)` (scoped to org). UI: Metadata card + "Package Method" and "Document Method" server actions with ledger entry redirection.

### 8. MQS Module (`/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus/noosphere/noosphere/evaluation/mqs.py:1-100`)

**Severity sub-score:** Part of five-criterion evaluation. `StubMqsJudge` (line:57-70) provides deterministic testing; production uses LLM judge via `judge(criterion, prompt)` protocol (line:48-53).
- Evidence serialized as JSON blob (line:659 in schema)
- Composite formula: `domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)` (line:29-31)

### 9. DB Connection from Python

**Pattern:** SQLModel (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy) with direct Postgres. Store (`noosphere/store.py:1-150`) uses:
- `SQLModel` base with `SQLField` + SQLAlchemy Column definitions
- `sqlmodel.Session` for transactions
- Models imported into `noosphere/models.py` (lines:29-102)
- No ORM layer; raw SQL only for complex joins
- `create_engine()` with pooling for Supabase pooler compatibility (line:140-150)

### 10. Test Structure

**Pattern:** Pytest fixtures + deterministic mocks. Example `test_mqs.py`:
- Fixture-based: `_profile(**overrides)` factory (line:30-42)
- Mock judge: `StubMqsJudge(responses={}, default_score=0.5)` (line:48-54)
- Assertions on JSON serialization and composite formula (line:45-81)
- No database needed for most tests (stub judge + in-memory data)

---

**Summary for Implementation:** You have SQLModel/Prisma dual-stack: Python side uses SQLModel for serialization/validation, Portal side uses Prisma ORM. Forecast prediction → resolution → MQS pipeline is established. Method registry is decorator-driven (name+version keyed). The new `ConclusionMethod` (M:N Conclusion↔Method) and `MethodTrackRecord` (1:1 Conclusion or ForecastPrediction linkage to track record) models will slot in as Prisma models with corresponding SQLModel mirrors in Python.

content.content:
1	"""CLI commands for the Method Registry subsystem."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	from typing import Optional
7	
8	import click
9	from rich.console import Console
10	from rich.table import Table
11	
12	console = Console()
13	
14	
15	def _get_store():
16	    from noosphere.cli import get_orchestrator
17	    return get_orchestrator(None).store
18	
19	
20	def _render(obj, as_json: bool) -> None:
21	    if as_json:
22	        click.echo(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, default=str))
23	
24	
25	@click.group("methods")
26	def cli() -> None:
27	    """Method registry: list, inspect, run, and diff registered methods."""
28	
29	
30	@cli.command("list")
31	@click.option("--status", "status_filter", type=str, default=None,
32	              help="Filter by status (experimental, active, deprecated, retired)")
33	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
34	def list_methods(status_filter: Optional[str], as_json: bool) -> None:
35	    """List all registered methods."""
36	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
37	
38	    methods = REGISTRY.list(status_filter=status_filter)
39	    if as_json:
40	        _render([{"name": m.name, "version": m.version, "status": m.status,
41	                  "type": m.method_type.value, "description": m.description}
42	                 for m in methods], True)
43	        return
44	    if not methods:
45	        console.print("[yellow]No methods registered.[/yellow]")
46	        return
47	    table = Table(title="Registered Methods", show_header=True)
48	    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
49	    table.add_column("Version")
50	    table.add_column("Status")
51	    table.add_column("Type")
52	    table.add_column("Description", max_width=50)
53	    for m in methods:
54	        table.add_row(m.name, m.version, m.status, m.method_type.value,
55	                      m.description[:50])
56	    console.print(table)
57	
58	
59	@cli.command("show")
60	@click.argument("ref")
61	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
62	def show_method(ref: str, as_json: bool) -> None:
63	    """Show details of a method. REF is name or name@version."""
64	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
65	
66	    name, version = (ref.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
67	    method, _fn = REGISTRY.get(name, version=version)
68	    if as_json:
69	        _render(method.model_dump(), True)
70	        return
71	    table = Table(title=f"Method: {method.name}@{method.version}", show_header=False)
72	    for field in ("method_id", "name", "version", "method_type", "status",
73	                  "owner", "description", "rationale"):
74	        val = getattr(method, field)
75	        table.add_row(field, str(val) if not hasattr(val, "value") else val.value)
76	    table.add_row("preconditions", ", ".join(method.preconditions) or "—")
77	    table.add_row("postconditions", ", ".join(method.postconditions) or "—")
78	    console.print(table)
79	
80	
81	@cli.command("run")
82	@click.argument("ref")
83	@click.option("--input", "input_json", required=True, help="JSON input for the method")
84	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
85	def run_method(ref: str, input_json: str, as_json: bool) -> None:
86	    """Run a method. REF is name or name@version."""
87	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
88	
89	    name, version = (ref.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
90	    _method, fn = REGISTRY.get(name, version=version)
91	    payload = json.loads(input_json)
92	    result = fn(payload)
93	    if as_json:
94	        _render(result if isinstance(result, (dict, list)) else str(result), True)
95	        return
96	    console.print(result)
97	
98	
99	@cli.command("diff")
100	@click.argument("ref_a")
101	@click.argument("ref_b")
102	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
103	def diff_methods(ref_a: str, ref_b: str, as_json: bool) -> None:
104	    """Diff two method versions. Each REF is name@version."""
105	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
106	
107	    name_a, ver_a = (ref_a.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
108	    name_b, ver_b = (ref_b.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
109	    m_a, _ = REGISTRY.get(name_a, version=ver_a)
110	    m_b, _ = REGISTRY.get(name_b, version=ver_b)
111	    d_a, d_b = m_a.model_dump(), m_b.model_dump()
112	    diffs = {k: {"old": d_a[k], "new": d_b[k]}
113	             for k in d_a if d_a.get(k) != d_b.get(k)}
114	    if as_json:
115	        _render(diffs, True)
116	        return
117	    if not diffs:
118	        console.print("[green]Methods are identical.[/green]")
119	        return
120	    table = Table(title=f"Diff: {ref_a} ↔ {ref_b}", show_header=True)
121	    table.add_column("Field", style="cyan")
122	    table.add_column("Old")
123	    table.add_column("New")
124	    for field, vals in diffs.items():
125	        table.add_row(field, str(vals["old"])[:60], str(vals["new"])[:60])
126	    console.print(table)
127	
128	
129	@cli.command("extract-candidates")
130	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
131	def extract_candidates(as_json: bool) -> None:
132	    """Scan the registry for candidate methods needing review."""
133	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
134	
135	    candidates = [m for m in REGISTRY.list() if m.status == "experimental"]
136	    if as_json:
137	        _render([{"name": m.name, "version": m.version, "owner": m.owner}
138	                 for m in candidates], True)
139	        return
140	    if not candidates:
141	        console.print("[green]No experimental candidates.[/green]")
142	        return
143	    table = Table(title="Method Candidates", show_header=True)
144	    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
145	    table.add_column("Version")
146	    table.add_column("Owner")
147	    for m in candidates:
148	        table.add_row(m.name, m.version, m.owner)
149	    console.print(table)
150

1	"""Methodology Quality Score (MQS): operational form of the five working
2	criteria from THE_META_METHOD.md.
3	
4	Each Conclusion that has at least one MethodologyProfile gets an MQS row with
5	five sub-scores in [0,1], an LLM-or-stub-judged evidence blob per sub-score,
6	and a composite that uses Domain Sensitivity as a multiplicative gate.
7	
8	The composite formula and sub-score rubrics are documented in
9	docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md. The doc and this module are kept in sync
10	by scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import json
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass
18	from datetime import datetime, timezone
19	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Protocol
20	
21	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
22	
23	
24	MQS_SCHEMA = "theseus.mqs.v1"
25	PROMPT_VERSION = "mqs-prompt-v1.0"
26	
27	# Mirrored verbatim by docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md and verified by
28	# scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
29	COMPOSITE_FORMULA = (
30	    "domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)"
31	)
32	
33	SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS: dict[str, float] = {
34	    "progressivity": 0.25,
35	    "severity": 0.25,
36	    "aim_method_fit": 0.25,
37	    "compressibility": 0.25,
38	}
39	
40	_DECISION_RULE_RE = re.compile(
41	    r"\b(if [a-z]+.*?then |we will |we'll |exit if |by [0-9]{4}|by q[1-4] |"
42	    r"check back |trigger |would falsif|will revisit)",
43	    re.IGNORECASE,
44	)
45	_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP = 600
46	
47	
48	class MqsJudge(Protocol):
49	    """LLM-judge contract. Tests pass a stub; production wires the real LLM."""
50	
51	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
52	        """Return a dict with at least {"score": float in [0,1], "rationale": str}."""
53	        ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class StubMqsJudge:
58	    """Deterministic judge used by tests and by the no-LLM backfill path.
59	
60	    `responses` lets a test pin a per-criterion score; otherwise every call
61	    returns `default_score` with rationale "stub".
62	    """
63	
64	    responses: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None = None
65	    default_score: float = 0.5
66	
67	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
68	        if self.responses and criterion in self.responses:
69	            return self.responses[criterion]
70	        return {"score": self.default_score, "rationale": "stub"}
71	
72	
73	class MethodologyProfileSummary(BaseModel):
74	    """Slimmed-down view of MethodologyProfile fields the scorer reads."""
75	
76	    pattern_type: str = ""
77	    title: str = ""
78	    summary: str = ""
79	    reasoning_moves: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
80	    transfer_targets: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
81	    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
82	    failure_modes: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
83	    confidence: float = 0.5
84	
85	
86	class MqsInput(BaseModel):
87	    """Everything the scorer needs about a conclusion to produce an MQS."""
88	
89	    conclusion_id: str
90	    conclusion_text: str = ""
91	    rationale: str = ""
92	    topic_hint: str = ""
93	    profiles: list[MethodologyProfileSummary] = Field(default_factory=list)
94	    forecast_count: int = 0
95	    has_check_back_date: bool = False
96	    dissent_claim_count: int = 0
97	
98	
99	class SubScore(BaseModel):
100	    score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
101	    evidence: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
102	
103	    @field_validator("evidence")
104	    @classmethod
105	    def _cap_strings(cls, value: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	        return _cap_evidence(value)
107	
108	
109	class MethodologyQualityScore(BaseModel):
110	    """Operational form of the five meta-method criteria for one conclusion."""
111	
112	    schema_: str = Field(default=MQS_SCHEMA, alias="schema")
113	    conclusion_id: str
114	    progressivity: SubScore
115	    severity: SubScore
116	    aim_method_fit: SubScore
117	    compressibility: SubScore
118	    domain_sensitivity: SubScore
119	    composite: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
120	    composite_formula: str = COMPOSITE_FORMULA
121	    model_name: str = "stub"
122	    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION
123	    scored_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
124	
125	    model_config = {"populate_by_name": True}
126	
127	
128	def _cap_evidence(value: Any) -> Any:
129	    if isinstance(value, str):
130	        return value[:_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP]
131	    if isinstance(value, list):
132	        return [_cap_evidence(item) for item in value]
133	    if isinstance(value, dict):
134	        return {k: _cap_evidence(v) for k, v in value.items()}
135	    return value
136	
137	
138	def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
139	    if x != x:  # NaN
140	        return 0.0
141	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))
142	
143	
144	def _decision_rule_phrase_count(text: str) -> int:
145	    return len(_DECISION_RULE_RE.findall(text or ""))
146	
147	
148	def _aggregate_profile_lists(
149	    profiles: Iterable[MethodologyProfileSummary],
150	    field: str,
151	) -> list[str]:
152	    out: list[str] = []
153	    for p in profiles:
154	        out.extend(getattr(p, field, []) or [])
155	    return out
156	
157	
158	def score_progressivity(input_data: MqsInput) -> SubScore:
159	    """Deterministic. Reads forecast count, check-back, decision-rule phrases."""
160	    text = f"{input_data.conclusion_text} {input_data.rationale}"
161	    rule_phrases = _decision_rule_phrase_count(text)
162	    forecasts = max(0, int(input_data.forecast_count))
163	    has_check_back = bool(input_data.has_check_back_date)
164	
165	    if has_check_back and forecasts >= 2 and rule_phrases >= 1:
166	        score = 1.00
167	    elif (has_check_back and forecasts >= 1) or forecasts >= 2:
168	        score = 0.85
169	    elif has_check_back or forecasts >= 1:
170	        score = 0.65
171	    elif rule_phrases >= 1:
172	        score = 0.40
173	    else:
174	        score = 0.00
175	
176	    return SubScore(
177	        score=score,
178	        evidence={
179	            "rule": "progressivity_v1",
180	            "forecast_count": forecasts,
181	            "has_check_back_date": has_check_back,
182	            "decision_rule_phrases": rule_phrases,
183	        },
184	    )
185	
186	
187	def score_severity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
188	    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
189	    dissent = max(0, int(input_data.dissent_claim_count))
190	
191	    floor = min(1.0, 0.15 * len(failure_modes) + 0.10 * dissent)
192	
193	    judged = judge.judge(
194	        criterion="severity",
195	        prompt={
196	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
197	            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
198	            "assumptions": _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions"),
199	            "dissent_claim_count": dissent,
200	        },
201	    )
202	    llm_score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
203	    score = max(floor, llm_score)
204	
205	    capped = False
206	    if not failure_modes and dissent == 0:
207	        score = min(score, 0.35)
208	        capped = True
209	
210	    return SubScore(
211	        score=score,
212	        evidence={
213	            "rule": "severity_v1",
214	            "deterministic_floor": round(floor, 4),
215	            "llm_score": round(llm_score, 4),
216	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
217	            "no_failure_modes_cap": capped,
218	        },
219	    )
220	
221	
222	def _topic_in_targets(topic_hint: str, targets: list[str]) -> bool:
223	    if not topic_hint:
224	        return True
225	    needle = topic_hint.strip().lower()
226	    if not needle:
227	        return True
228	    root = re.split(r"\W+", needle)[0]
229	    if not root:
230	        return True
231	    return any(root in (t or "").lower() for t in targets)
232	
233	
234	def score_aim_method_fit(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
235	    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
236	    pattern_types = [p.pattern_type for p in input_data.profiles]
237	
238	    judged = judge.judge(
239	        criterion="aim_method_fit",
240	        prompt={
241	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
242	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
243	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
244	            "pattern_types": pattern_types,
245	        },
246	    )
247	    score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
248	
249	    penalty = 0.0
250	    if input_data.topic_hint and not _topic_in_targets(
251	        input_data.topic_hint, transfer_targets
252	    ):
253	        penalty = 0.10
254	        score = max(0.0, score - penalty)
255	
256	    return SubScore(
257	        score=score,
258	        evidence={
259	            "rule": "aim_method_fit_v1",
260	            "llm_score": round(_clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0)), 4),
261	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
262	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
263	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
264	            "topic_outside_targets_penalty": penalty,
265	        },
266	    )
267	
268	
269	def score_compressibility(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
270	    assumptions = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions")
271	    n = len(assumptions)
272	
273	    judged = judge.judge(
274	        criterion="compressibility",
275	        prompt={
276	            "assumptions": assumptions,
277	            "reasoning_moves": _aggregate_profile_lists(
278	                input_data.profiles, "reasoning_moves"
279	            ),
280	        },
281	    )
282	    decorative = int(judged.get("decorative_count", 0) or 0)
283	    decorative = max(0, min(decorative, n))
284	    effective_n = max(1, n - decorative)
285	
286	    base = 1.0 / (1.0 + max(0, effective_n - 1) * 0.25)
287	    score = _clamp01(base)
288	
289	    return SubScore(
290	        score=score,
291	        evidence={
292	            "rule": "compressibility_v1",
293	            "assumption_count": n,
294	            "decorative_count": decorative,
295	            "effective_n": effective_n,
296	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
297	        },
298	    )
299	
300	
301	def score_domain_sensitivity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
302	    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
303	    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
304	
305	    judged = judge.judge(
306	        criterion="domain_sensitivity",
307	        prompt={
308	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
309	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
310	            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
311	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
312	        },
313	    )
314	    raw = judged.get("score", None)
315	    if raw is None:
316	        # Backfill default: uncertain, not failed.
317	        score = 0.5
318	        used_default = True
319	    else:
320	        score = _clamp01(raw)
321	        used_default = False
322	
323	    floor = 0.10 if not failure_modes else 0.0
324	    score = max(score, floor)
325	
326	    return SubScore(
327	        score=score,
328	        evidence={
329	            "rule": "domain_sensitivity_v1",
330	            "llm_score": None if used_default else round(_clamp01(raw), 4),
331	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
332	            "no_failure_modes_floor": floor,
333	            "used_uncertain_default": used_default,
334	        },
335	    )
336	
337	
338	def composite_score(
339	    *,
340	    progressivity: float,
341	    severity: float,
342	    aim_method_fit: float,
343	    compressibility: float,
344	    domain_sensitivity: float,
345	) -> float:
346	    mean_other = (
347	        SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["progressivity"] * _clamp01(progressivity)
348	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["severity"] * _clamp01(severity)
349	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["aim_method_fit"] * _clamp01(aim_method_fit)
350	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["compressibility"] * _clamp01(compressibility)
351	    )
352	    return _clamp01(_clamp01(domain_sensitivity) * mean_other)
353	
354	
355	def score_conclusion(
356	    input_data: MqsInput,
357	    *,
358	    judge: Optional[MqsJudge] = None,
359	    model_name: str = "stub",
360	    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION,
361	) -> MethodologyQualityScore:
362	    """Produce a complete MQS for one conclusion. Composable with the peer-
363	    review swarm: callers may inject a real LLM judge or pass `judge=None` to
364	    use the deterministic stub (used by the backfill CLI when no LLM is
365	    configured)."""
366	    judge_ = judge or StubMqsJudge()
367	
368	    prog = score_progressivity(input_data)
369	    sev = score_severity(input_data, judge_)
370	    fit = score_aim_method_fit(input_data, judge_)
371	    comp = score_compressibility(input_data, judge_)
372	    dom = score_domain_sensitivity(input_data, judge_)
373	
374	    composite = composite_score(
375	        progressivity=prog.score,
376	        severity=sev.score,
377	        aim_method_fit=fit.score,
378	        compressibility=comp.score,
379	        domain_sensitivity=dom.score,
380	    )
381	
382	    return MethodologyQualityScore(
383	        conclusion_id=input_data.conclusion_id,
384	        progressivity=prog,
385	        severity=sev,
386	        aim_method_fit=fit,
387	        compressibility=comp,
388	        domain_sensitivity=dom,
389	        composite=composite,
390	        model_name=model_name,
391	        prompt_version=prompt_version,
392	    )
393	
394	
395	def evidence_payload(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> dict[str, Any]:
396	    """Build the JSON blob persisted in `MethodologyQualityScore.evidence`.
397	
398	    Stable shape so a human reviewer can contest a sub-score and so the doc
399	    consistency check can verify keys."""
400	    return {
401	        "schema": MQS_SCHEMA,
402	        "composite_formula": COMPOSITE_FORMULA,
403	        "subscore_weights": SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS,
404	        "progressivity": score.progressivity.evidence,
405	        "severity": score.severity.evidence,
406	        "aim_method_fit": score.aim_method_fit.evidence,
407	        "compressibility": score.compressibility.evidence,
408	        "domain_sensitivity": score.domain_sensitivity.evidence,
409	    }
410	
411	
412	def evidence_payload_json(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> str:
413	    return json.dumps(evidence_payload(score), sort_keys=True, default=str)

415	
416	__all__ = [
417	    "COMPOSITE_FORMULA",
418	    "MQS_SCHEMA",
419	    "PROMPT_VERSION",
420	    "SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS",
421	    "MethodologyProfileSummary",

"""Methodology Quality Score (MQS): operational form of the five working
criteria from THE_META_METHOD.md.

Each Conclusion that has at least one MethodologyProfile gets an MQS row with
five sub-scores in [0,1], an LLM-or-stub-judged evidence blob per sub-score,
and a composite that uses Domain Sensitivity as a multiplicative gate.

The composite formula and sub-score rubrics are documented in
docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md. The doc and this module are kept in sync
by scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Protocol

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator


MQS_SCHEMA = "theseus.mqs.v1"
PROMPT_VERSION = "mqs-prompt-v1.0"

# Mirrored verbatim by docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md and verified by
# scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
COMPOSITE_FORMULA = (
    "domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)"
)

SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS: dict[str, float] = {
    "progressivity": 0.25,
    "severity": 0.25,
    "aim_method_fit": 0.25,
    "compressibility": 0.25,
}

_DECISION_RULE_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(if [a-z]+.*?then |we will |we'll |exit if |by [0-9]{4}|by q[1-4] |"
    r"check back |trigger |would falsif|will revisit)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)
_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP = 600


class MqsJudge(Protocol):
    """LLM-judge contract. Tests pass a stub; production wires the real LLM."""

    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a dict with at least {"score": float in [0,1], "rationale": str}."""
        ...


@dataclass
class StubMqsJudge:
    """Deterministic judge used by tests and by the no-LLM backfill path.

    `responses` lets a test pin a per-criterion score; otherwise every call
    returns `default_score` with rationale "stub".
    """

    responses: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None = None
    default_score: float = 0.5

    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        if self.responses and criterion in self.responses:
            return self.responses[criterion]
        return {"score": self.default_score, "rationale": "stub"}


class MethodologyProfileSummary(BaseModel):
    """Slimmed-down view of MethodologyProfile fields the scorer reads."""

    pattern_type: str = ""
    title: str = ""
    summary: str = ""
    reasoning_moves: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    transfer_targets: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    failure_modes: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    confidence: float = 0.5


class MqsInput(BaseModel):
    """Everything the scorer needs about a conclusion to produce an MQS."""

    conclusion_id: str
    conclusion_text: str = ""
    rationale: str = ""
    topic_hint: str = ""
    profiles: list[MethodologyProfileSummary] = Field(default_factory=list)
    forecast_count: int = 0
    has_check_back_date: bool = False
    dissent_claim_count: int = 0


class SubScore(BaseModel):
    score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    evidence: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)

    @field_validator("evidence")
    @classmethod
    def _cap_strings(cls, value: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return _cap_evidence(value)


class MethodologyQualityScore(BaseModel):
    """Operational form of the five meta-method criteria for one conclusion."""

    schema_: str = Field(default=MQS_SCHEMA, alias="schema")
    conclusion_id: str
    progressivity: SubScore
    severity: SubScore
    aim_method_fit: SubScore
    compressibility: SubScore
    domain_sensitivity: SubScore
    composite: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    composite_formula: str = COMPOSITE_FORMULA
    model_name: str = "stub"
    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION
    scored_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    model_config = {"populate_by_name": True}


def _cap_evidence(value: Any) -> Any:
    if isinstance(value, str):
        return value[:_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP]
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return [_cap_evidence(item) for item in value]
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return {k: _cap_evidence(v) for k, v in value.items()}
    return value


def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
    if x != x:  # NaN
        return 0.0
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))


def _decision_rule_phrase_count(text: str) -> int:
    return len(_DECISION_RULE_RE.findall(text or ""))


def _aggregate_profile_lists(
    profiles: Iterable[MethodologyProfileSummary],
    field: str,
) -> list[str]:
    out: list[str] = []
    for p in profiles:
        out.extend(getattr(p, field, []) or [])
    return out


def score_progressivity(input_data: MqsInput) -> SubScore:
    """Deterministic. Reads forecast count, check-back, decision-rule phrases."""
    text = f"{input_data.conclusion_text} {input_data.rationale}"
    rule_phrases = _decision_rule_phrase_count(text)
    forecasts = max(0, int(input_data.forecast_count))
    has_check_back = bool(input_data.has_check_back_date)

    if has_check_back and forecasts >= 2 and rule_phrases >= 1:
        score = 1.00
    elif (has_check_back and forecasts >= 1) or forecasts >= 2:
        score = 0.85
    elif has_check_back or forecasts >= 1:
        score = 0.65
    elif rule_phrases >= 1:
        score = 0.40
    else:
        score = 0.00

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "progressivity_v1",
            "forecast_count": forecasts,
            "has_check_back_date": has_check_back,
            "decision_rule_phrases": rule_phrases,
        },
    )


def score_severity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
    dissent = max(0, int(input_data.dissent_claim_count))

    floor = min(1.0, 0.15 * len(failure_modes) + 0.10 * dissent)

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="severity",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
            "assumptions": _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions"),
            "dissent_claim_count": dissent,
        },
    )
    llm_score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
    score = max(floor, llm_score)

    capped = False
    if not failure_modes and dissent == 0:
        score = min(score, 0.35)
        capped = True

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "severity_v1",
            "deterministic_floor": round(floor, 4),
            "llm_score": round(llm_score, 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "no_failure_modes_cap": capped,
        },
    )


def _topic_in_targets(topic_hint: str, targets: list[str]) -> bool:
    if not topic_hint:
        return True
    needle = topic_hint.strip().lower()
    if not needle:
        return True
    root = re.split(r"\W+", needle)[0]
    if not root:
        return True
    return any(root in (t or "").lower() for t in targets)


def score_aim_method_fit(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
    pattern_types = [p.pattern_type for p in input_data.profiles]

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="aim_method_fit",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
            "pattern_types": pattern_types,
        },
    )
    score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))

    penalty = 0.0
    if input_data.topic_hint and not _topic_in_targets(
        input_data.topic_hint, transfer_targets
    ):
        penalty = 0.10
        score = max(0.0, score - penalty)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "aim_method_fit_v1",
            "llm_score": round(_clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0)), 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
            "topic_outside_targets_penalty": penalty,
        },
    )


def score_compressibility(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    assumptions = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions")
    n = len(assumptions)

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="compressibility",
        prompt={
            "assumptions": assumptions,
            "reasoning_moves": _aggregate_profile_lists(
                input_data.profiles, "reasoning_moves"
            ),
        },
    )
    decorative = int(judged.get("decorative_count", 0) or 0)
    decorative = max(0, min(decorative, n))
    effective_n = max(1, n - decorative)

    base = 1.0 / (1.0 + max(0, effective_n - 1) * 0.25)
    score = _clamp01(base)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "compressibility_v1",
            "assumption_count": n,
            "decorative_count": decorative,
            "effective_n": effective_n,
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
        },
    )


def score_domain_sensitivity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="domain_sensitivity",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
        },
    )
    raw = judged.get("score", None)
    if raw is None:
        # Backfill default: uncertain, not failed.
        score = 0.5
        used_default = True
    else:
        score = _clamp01(raw)
        used_default = False

    floor = 0.10 if not failure_modes else 0.0
    score = max(score, floor)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "domain_sensitivity_v1",
            "llm_score": None if used_default else round(_clamp01(raw), 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "no_failure_modes_floor": floor,
            "used_uncertain_default": used_default,
        },
    )


def composite_score(
    *,
    progressivity: float,
    severity: float,
    aim_method_fit: float,
    compressibility: float,
    domain_sensitivity: float,
) -> float:
    mean_other = (
        SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["progressivity"] * _clamp01(progressivity)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["severity"] * _clamp01(severity)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["aim_method_fit"] * _clamp01(aim_method_fit)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["compressibility"] * _clamp01(compressibility)
    )
    return _clamp01(_clamp01(domain_sensitivity) * mean_other)


def score_conclusion(
    input_data: MqsInput,
    *,
    judge: Optional[MqsJudge] = None,
    model_name: str = "stub",
    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION,
) -> MethodologyQualityScore:
    """Produce a complete MQS for one conclusion. Composable with the peer-
    review swarm: callers may inject a real LLM judge or pass `judge=None` to
    use the deterministic stub (used by the backfill CLI when no LLM is
    configured)."""
    judge_ = judge or StubMqsJudge()

    prog = score_progressivity(input_data)
    sev = score_severity(input_data, judge_)
    fit = score_aim_method_fit(input_data, judge_)
    comp = score_compressibility(input_data, judge_)
    dom = score_domain_sensitivity(input_data, judge_)

    composite = composite_score(
        progressivity=prog.score,
        severity=sev.score,
        aim_method_fit=fit.score,
        compressibility=comp.score,
        domain_sensitivity=dom.score,
    )

    return MethodologyQualityScore(
        conclusion_id=input_data.conclusion_id,
        progressivity=prog,
        severity=sev,
        aim_method_fit=fit,
        compressibility=comp,
        domain_sensitivity=dom,
        composite=composite,
        model_name=model_name,
        prompt_version=prompt_version,
    )


def evidence_payload(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build the JSON blob persisted in `MethodologyQualityScore.evidence`.

    Stable shape so a human reviewer can contest a sub-score and so the doc
    consistency check can verify keys."""
    return {
        "schema": MQS_SCHEMA,
        "composite_formula": COMPOSITE_FORMULA,
        "subscore_weights": SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS,
        "progressivity": score.progressivity.evidence,
        "severity": score.severity.evidence,
        "aim_method_fit": score.aim_method_fit.evidence,
        "compressibility": score.compressibility.evidence,
        "domain_sensitivity": score.domain_sensitivity.evidence,
    }


def evidence_payload_json(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> str:
    return json.dumps(evidence_payload(score), sort_keys=True, default=str)


__all__ = [
    "COMPOSITE_FORMULA",
    "MQS_SCHEMA",
    "PROMPT_VERSION",
    "SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS",
    "MethodologyProfileSummary",
    "MethodologyQualityScore",
    "MqsInput",
    "MqsJudge",
    "StubMqsJudge",
    "SubScore",
    "composite_score",
    "evidence_payload",
    "evidence_payload_json",
    "score_aim_method_fit",
    "score_compressibility",
    "score_conclusion",
    "score_domain_sensitivity",
    "score_progressivity",
    "score_severity",
]

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation, Method
6	
7	
8	class MethodCollisionError(Exception):
9	    pass
10	
11	
12	class MethodNotFoundError(Exception):
13	    pass
14	
15	
16	def _parse_semver(version: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
17	    parts: list[int] = []
18	    for p in version.split("."):
19	        try:
20	            parts.append(int(p))
21	        except ValueError:
22	            parts.append(0)
23	    return tuple(parts)
24	
25	
26	class MethodRegistry:
27	    def __init__(self) -> None:
28	        self._specs: dict[tuple[str, str], Method] = {}
29	        self._fns: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable] = {}
30	        self._emits_edges: dict[str, list[CascadeEdgeRelation]] = {}
31	
32	    def register(self, spec: Method, fn: Callable) -> None:
33	        key = (spec.name, spec.version)
34	        if key in self._specs:
35	            raise MethodCollisionError(
36	                f"Method {spec.name} v{spec.version} already registered"
37	            )
38	        self._specs[key] = spec
39	        self._fns[key] = fn
40	
41	    def set_emits_edges(
42	        self, method_id: str, edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]
43	    ) -> None:
44	        self._emits_edges[method_id] = list(edges)
45	
46	    def get_emits_edges(self, method_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdgeRelation]:
47	        return self._emits_edges.get(method_id, [])
48	
49	    def get(
50	        self,
51	        name: str,
52	        version: str = "latest",
53	        include_retired: bool = False,
54	    ) -> tuple[Method, Callable]:
55	        if version != "latest":
56	            key = (name, version)
57	            if key not in self._specs:
58	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
59	                    f"Method {name} v{version} not found"
60	                )
61	            spec = self._specs[key]
62	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
63	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
64	                    f"Method {name} v{version} is retired"
65	                )
66	            return spec, self._fns[key]
67	
68	        candidates: list[tuple[str, Method]] = []
69	        for (n, v), spec in self._specs.items():
70	            if n != name:
71	                continue
72	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
73	                continue
74	            candidates.append((v, spec))
75	
76	        if not candidates:
77	            raise MethodNotFoundError(f"No active method found for {name}")
78	
79	        candidates.sort(key=lambda x: _parse_semver(x[0]), reverse=True)
80	        best_version = candidates[0][0]
81	        return self._specs[(name, best_version)], self._fns[(name, best_version)]
82	
83	    def list(self, status_filter: str | None = None) -> list[Method]:
84	        specs = list(self._specs.values())
85	        if status_filter is not None:
86	            specs = [s for s in specs if s.status == status_filter]
87	        return specs
88	
89	
90	REGISTRY = MethodRegistry()
91

136:  conclusions          Conclusion[]
144:  publishedConclusions PublishedConclusion[]
149:  methodologyProfiles  MethodologyProfile[]
150:  methodologyQualityScores MethodologyQualityScore[]
153:  forecastMarkets      ForecastMarket[]
154:  forecastPredictions  ForecastPrediction[]
201:  attributedConclusions      Conclusion[]                @relation("ConclusionFounder")
206:  conclusionDeletionRequests ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
350:  // Derived entities (Conclusions via the M:N join, Contradictions,
360:  conclusionSources   ConclusionSource[]
361:  methodologyProfiles MethodologyProfile[]
430:// ── Conclusion — mirrors noosphere.models.Conclusion ───────────────────────
432:model Conclusion {
443:  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
445:  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
470:  attributedFounder   Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])

content.content:
420	  respondedAt DateTime?
421	
422	  upload    Upload  @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
423	  requester Founder @relation(fields: [requesterId], references: [id])
424	
425	  @@index([uploadId])
426	  @@index([requesterId])
427	  @@index([status])
428	}
429	
430	// ── Conclusion — mirrors noosphere.models.Conclusion ───────────────────────
431	
432	model Conclusion {
433	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
434	  organizationId String
435	
436	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
437	  noosphereId String? @unique
438	  text        String
439	
440	  // ── Dedup key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
441	  // `normalizedText` is `LOWER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(text, '\s+', ' ')))`
442	  // computed once at ingest time. The ingest pipeline checks for an
443	  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
444	  // if one exists, it adds the current upload as an ADDITIONAL source
445	  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
446	  // what makes deletion correct: when two uploads produced the same
447	  // claim and we delete one, the conclusion stays — it's still
448	  // supported by the other source.
449	  //
450	  // Nullable so the column can be added to an existing DB without a
451	  // rewrite; the migration back-fills every existing row. Indexed
452	  // `(organizationId, normalizedText)` because every lookup is scoped
453	  // to a single org.
454	  normalizedText         String?
455	  confidenceTier         String // firm | founder | open (ConfidenceTier)
456	  rationale              String   @default("")
457	  supportingPrincipleIds String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
458	  evidenceChainClaimIds  String   @default("[]")
459	  dissentClaimIds        String   @default("[]")
460	  confidence             Float    @default(0)
461	  topicHint              String   @default("") // UI filter / display
462	  createdAt              DateTime @default(now())
463	
464	  /// JSON-serialized float[] embedding produced by Noosphere's sentence
465	  /// encoder at ingest time. Null for conclusions ingested before this
466	  /// column was added; the semantic explorer backfills on demand.
467	  embeddingJson String?
468	
469	  attributedFounderId String?
470	  attributedFounder   Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])
471	  organization        Organization                @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
472	  publicationReviews  PublicationReview[]
473	  sources             ConclusionSource[]
474	  methodologyProfiles MethodologyProfile[]
475	  /// Optional MQS row (1:1). Built by `noosphere mqs backfill`; re-runnable
476	  /// without rewriting the underlying methodology profile.
477	  methodologyQualityScore MethodologyQualityScore?
478	  deletionRequests    ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
479	  dashboardDismissals DashboardDismissal[]
480	
481	  /// Conclusion edit timestamp; the public MQS pill is only shown when the
482	  /// MQS row's `scoredAt` is at least this recent.
483	  updatedAt           DateTime?                   @updatedAt
484	
485	  @@index([organizationId])
486	  @@index([organizationId, normalizedText])
487	}
488	
489	/// Many-to-many bridge Conclusion ↔ Upload.
490	///
491	/// Every conclusion that came out of the ingest pipeline has at least
492	/// one row here tying it back to the upload it was extracted from.
493	/// When the SAME normalized text appears in multiple uploads, the
494	/// ingest pipeline reuses the existing Conclusion row and adds a new
495	/// ConclusionSource row per additional upload, so the upstream count
496	/// can drop from 2 → 1 → 0 as uploads are deleted.
497	///
498	/// Delete semantics (enforced by the app-side delete handlers, NOT by
499	/// FK cascade — we soft-delete uploads, so the FK's ON DELETE CASCADE
500	/// would not fire anyway):
501	///
502	///   * When an upload is soft-deleted, every ConclusionSource row
503	///     with that uploadId is hard-deleted.
504	///   * After that, any Conclusion with zero remaining rows is
505	///     hard-deleted too (the claim's last source is gone).
506	///   * Conclusions with sources from other uploads remain, just
507	///     with one fewer row.
508	///
509	/// The FK cascade below still serves as a safety net for the rare
510	/// case of a hard-delete (org takedown, privacy-law purge).
511	model ConclusionSource {
512	  conclusionId String
513	  uploadId     String
514	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
515	
516	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
517	  upload     Upload     @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
518	
519	  @@id([conclusionId, uploadId])
520	  @@index([conclusionId])
521	  @@index([uploadId])
522	}
523	
524	// ── Publication (SP10) — review queue + versioned public snapshots ───────────
525	
526	/// Internal gate before a conclusion may appear on the public site.
527	model PublicationReview {
528	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
529	  organizationId String
530	  conclusionId   String
531	
532	  /// queued | in_review | needs_revision | declined | published
533	  status        String @default("queued")
534	  /// JSON: { metaAnalysisOk, adversarialEngagedOk, clarityOk, noLeakageOk }
535	  checklistJson String @default("{}")
536	  reviewerNotes String @default("")
537	  declineReason String @default("")
538	  revisionAsk   String @default("")
539	
540	  reviewerFounderId String?
541	  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
542	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
543	
544	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
545	  target       Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
546	  reviewer     Founder?     @relation("PublicationReviewer", fields: [reviewerFounderId], references: [id])
547	
548	  @@index([organizationId])
549	  @@index([conclusionId])
550	  @@index([status])
551	}
552	
553	/// Immutable-ish public snapshot (new row per material revision).
554	model PublishedConclusion {
555	  id                 String @id @default(cuid())
556	  organizationId     String
557	  sourceConclusionId String
558	
559	  slug    String
560	  version Int    @default(1)
561	  /// CONCLUSION for reviewed firm conclusions; ARTICLE for generated longform essays.
562	  kind    String @default("CONCLUSION")
563	
564	  /// Headline discounted confidence (SP05); stated founder/model confidence alongside in JSON.
565	  discountedConfidence      Float
566	  statedConfidence          Float  @default(0)
567	  calibrationDiscountReason String @default("")
568	
569	  /// Full page payload: evidence, exit conditions, adversarial, voice map, timeline, citations.
570	  payloadJson String @default("{}")
571	
572	  /// Zenodo DOI for this revision (minted on first publish of revision).
573	  doi            String @default("")
574	  zenodoRecordId String @default("")
575	
576	  publishedAt DateTime @default(now())
577	
578	  organization Organization     @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
579	  responses    PublicResponse[]
580	
581	  @@unique([slug, version])
582	  @@index([organizationId])
583	  @@index([slug])
584	  @@index([kind, publishedAt])
585	}
586	
587	// ── Methodology profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
588	
589	/// A durable description of how a piece of material reasons, not merely what it
590	/// concludes. Noosphere writes these from transcripts/uploads and reanalysis
591	/// scripts; publication review can attach them to public conclusions.
592	model MethodologyProfile {
593	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
594	  organizationId String
595	  uploadId       String?
596	  conclusionId   String?
597	
598	  /// UPLOAD when derived from a transcript/artifact; CONCLUSION when derived
599	  /// from a conclusion/publication review; MANUAL for founder-authored method
600	  /// summaries.
601	  sourceKind String @default("UPLOAD")
602	
603	  patternType String
604	  title       String
605	  summary     String @db.Text
606	
607	  reasoningMoves  Json
608	  transferTargets Json
609	  assumptions     Json
610	  failureModes    Json
611	  evidenceAnchors Json
612	
613	  confidence Float @default(0.5)
614	  dedupeKey  String
615	
616	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
617	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
618	
619	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
620	  upload       Upload?      @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
621	  conclusion   Conclusion?  @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
622	
623	  @@unique([organizationId, dedupeKey])
624	  @@index([organizationId, createdAt])
625	  @@index([organizationId, patternType])
626	  @@index([uploadId])
627	  @@index([conclusionId])
628	}
629	
630	// ── Methodology Quality Score (MQS) ─────────────────────────────────────────
631	
632	/// Operational form of the five working criteria from `THE_META_METHOD.md`:
633	/// progressivity, severity, aim-method fit, compressibility, domain sensitivity.
634	///
635	/// One row per Conclusion. The score is additive — it never silently mutates a
636	/// MethodologyProfile — and re-runnable: `noosphere mqs backfill` overwrites
637	/// the existing row in place using the conclusion id as the unique key.
638	///
639	/// The composite is intentionally not a simple mean. Domain Sensitivity acts
640	/// as a multiplicative gate; the canonical formula is documented in
641	/// `docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md` and verified by
642	/// `scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py`.
643	model MethodologyQualityScore {
644	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
645	  organizationId String
646	
647	  /// 1:1 with Conclusion. The unique constraint enforces the relation.
648	  conclusionId String @unique
649	
650	  progressivity     Float
651	  severity          Float
652	  aimMethodFit      Float
653	  compressibility   Float
654	  domainSensitivity Float
655	  composite         Float
656	
657	  /// JSON blob with one entry per sub-score plus the composite formula and
658	  /// schema version, so a human reviewer can inspect and contest the score.
659	  evidence Json
660	
661	  /// Identity of the producer so re-runs can be reasoned about (e.g.
662	  /// "anthropic/claude-opus-4-7" + "mqs-prompt-v1.0").
663	  modelName     String @default("stub")
664	  promptVersion String @default("mqs-prompt-v1.0")
665	
666	  scoredAt  DateTime @default(now())
667	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
668	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
669	
670	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
671	  conclusion   Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
672	
673	  @@index([organizationId, scoredAt])
674	  @@index([organizationId, composite])
675	}
676	
677	// ── Currents — observed external events + source-grounded opinions ──────────
678	
679	model CurrentEvent {
680	  id              String             @id @default(cuid())
681	  organizationId  String
682	  source          CurrentEventSource
683	  externalId      String
684	  authorHandle    String?
685	  text            String             @db.Text
686	  url             String?
687	  capturedAt      DateTime           @default(now())
688	  observedAt      DateTime
689	  topicHint       String?
690	  isNearDuplicate Boolean            @default(false)
691	  embedding       Bytes?
692	  metrics         Json?
693	  status          CurrentEventStatus @default(OBSERVED)
694	  dedupeHash      String             @unique
695	  createdAt       DateTime           @default(now())
696	  updatedAt       DateTime           @updatedAt
697	
698	  organization Organization   @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
699	  opinions     EventOpinion[]
700	
701	  @@index([organizationId, observedAt])
702	  @@index([organizationId, status])
703	}
704	
705	model EventOpinion {
706	  id               String            @id @default(cuid())
707	  organizationId   String
708	  eventId          String
709	  stance           OpinionStance
710	  confidence       Float
711	  headline         String            @db.VarChar(140)
712	  bodyMarkdown     String            @db.Text
713	  uncertaintyNotes String[]
714	  topicHint        String?
715	  modelName        String
716	  promptTokens     Int               @default(0)
717	  completionTokens Int               @default(0)
718	  abstentionReason AbstentionReason?
719	  generatedAt      DateTime          @default(now())

820	}
821	
822	// ── Forecasts — external prediction markets + source-grounded predictions ───
823	
824	model ForecastMarket {
825	  id                 String               @id @default(cuid())
826	  organizationId     String
827	  source             ForecastSource
828	  externalId         String
829	  title              String               @db.VarChar(280)
830	  description        String?              @db.Text
831	  resolutionCriteria String?              @db.Text
832	  category           String?
833	  currentYesPrice    Decimal?             @db.Decimal(8, 6)
834	  currentNoPrice     Decimal?             @db.Decimal(8, 6)
835	  volume             Decimal?             @db.Decimal(18, 4)
836	  openTime           DateTime?
837	  closeTime          DateTime?
838	  resolvedAt         DateTime?
839	  resolvedOutcome    ForecastOutcome?
840	  rawPayload         Json
841	  status             ForecastMarketStatus @default(OPEN)
842	  createdAt          DateTime             @default(now())
843	  updatedAt          DateTime             @updatedAt
844	
845	  organization Organization         @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
846	  predictions  ForecastPrediction[]
847	  traces       ForecastTrace[]
848	
849	  @@unique([source, externalId])
850	  @@index([organizationId, status, closeTime])
851	  @@index([source, category])
852	  @@index([updatedAt])
853	}
854	
855	model WatchedMarket {
856	  id                 String         @id @default(cuid())
857	  organizationId     String
858	  source             ForecastSource
859	  url                String         @db.Text
860	  externalId         String?
861	  status             String         @default("ACTIVE")
862	  notes              String?        @db.Text
863	  lastConsideredAt   DateTime?
864	  createdAt          DateTime       @default(now())
865	  updatedAt          DateTime       @updatedAt
866	
867	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
868	
869	  @@unique([organizationId, url])
870	  @@index([organizationId, status, createdAt])
871	  @@index([source, status])
872	}
873	
874	model ForecastPrediction {
875	  id               String                   @id @default(cuid())
876	  marketId         String
877	  organizationId   String
878	  probabilityYes   Decimal?                 @db.Decimal(8, 6)
879	  confidenceLow    Decimal?                 @db.Decimal(8, 6)
880	  confidenceHigh   Decimal?                 @db.Decimal(8, 6)
881	  headline         String                   @db.VarChar(140)
882	  reasoning        String                   @db.Text
883	  status           ForecastPredictionStatus
884	  abstentionReason String?
885	  topicHint        String?
886	  modelName        String
887	  promptTokens     Int                      @default(0)
888	  completionTokens Int                      @default(0)
889	  liveAuthorizedAt DateTime?
890	  liveAuthorizedBy String?
891	  createdAt        DateTime                 @default(now())
892	  updatedAt        DateTime                 @updatedAt
893	
894	  market           ForecastMarket            @relation(fields: [marketId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
895	  organization     Organization              @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
896	  citations        ForecastCitation[]
897	  resolution       ForecastResolution?
898	  bets             ForecastBet[]
899	  trace            ForecastTrace?
900	  followUpSessions ForecastFollowUpSession[]
901	
902	  @@index([organizationId, status, createdAt])
903	  @@index([marketId, createdAt])
904	  @@index([liveAuthorizedAt])
905	}
906	
907	model ForecastTrace {
908	  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
909	  predictionId    String   @unique
910	  marketId        String
911	  organizationId  String
912	  marketTitle     String   @db.VarChar(280)
913	  principlesUsed  Json
914	  modelOutput     Json
915	  gateResults     Json
916	  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
917	  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt
918	
919	  prediction   ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
920	  market       ForecastMarket     @relation(fields: [marketId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
921	  organization Organization       @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
922	
923	  @@index([marketId])
924	  @@index([organizationId, createdAt])
925	}
926	
927	model ForecastCitation {
928	  id             String               @id @default(cuid())
929	  predictionId   String
930	  sourceType     String
931	  sourceId       String
932	  quotedSpan     String               @db.Text
933	  supportLabel   ForecastSupportLabel
934	  retrievalScore Float?
935	  isRevoked      Boolean              @default(false)
936	  revokedReason  String?
937	  createdAt      DateTime             @default(now())
938	
939	  prediction ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
940	
941	  @@index([predictionId])
942	  @@index([sourceType, sourceId])
943	}
944	
945	model ForecastResolution {
946	  id                String          @id @default(cuid())
947	  predictionId      String          @unique
948	  marketOutcome     ForecastOutcome
949	  brierScore        Float?
950	  logLoss           Float?
951	  calibrationBucket Decimal?        @db.Decimal(3, 1)
952	  resolvedAt        DateTime
953	  justification     String          @db.Text
954	  rawSettlement     Json?
955	  createdAt         DateTime        @default(now())
956	
957	  prediction ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
958	
959	  @@index([resolvedAt])
960	  @@index([calibrationBucket])
961	}
962	
963	model ForecastBet {
